subject: The interior of an impossible, warped house in which a curving staircase ascends past multiple impossibly-oriented rooms — each chamber visible through fractured wall openings shows a different scene including stormy seas, fiery skies, draped sheeted forms, and a chamber lit by pink-magenta points of light
description: A surreal cutaway view of a house interior where architecture, gravity, and perspective have come unstuck. A pale stone or wood staircase curves up the center-left of the frame, banister rendered in soft pinks and whites. Visible through fractured wall apertures are multiple rooms occupying conflicting orientations: at upper-left a curved window or tank-like chamber shows churning blue-black storm clouds with white-curl detail; upper-right a chamber framed by warped beams glows in orange and coral, its back wall obscured by ragged red-pink curtain or vegetation; right-of-center a corridor sits behind a row of pale, vertically-draped sheet-like forms hanging in pillar arrangement; lower-right a stone-flagged chamber is studded with rows of small pink-magenta points of light reading as candle flames or eyes. Soft violet, dusty rose, coral, sage, and pale teal dominate. The overall feeling is dollhouse-cross-section meets liminal-space horror. No clearly identifiable figures are depicted, though the row of draped white forms suggests sheeted humanoid silhouettes.
foreground: curving staircase and banister rendered in pale pinks and whites
background: fractured wall openings revealing storm-cloud chamber, fire-lit chamber, draped sheet-forms, and pink-candle-lit chamber
